title: Ironing Board Holder
summary: A wall-mounted shelf with two dowel pegs that hold an ironing board flat against the wall, plus a bar for hanging the iron.
description:
The dowels are what actually bear the ironing board's weight, so the trick to a clean, sturdy result is drilling the screw holes all the way through the plywood face first, before flipping the piece over to install the dowels from the back. Predrilling from the front lets you place each dowel exactly where you marked it and drive the screw straight and centered, instead of guessing blind from the back and hoping the screw lands in the middle of a dowel you can't see.

Everything else is basic shelf-and-cleat construction: a plywood back panel, a small shelf near the bottom for the iron itself, and a metal or wood bar across the front to hold it. This is a genuinely flexible build — you can join every part with brad nails and glue, self-tapping screws, or pocket screws, whichever tools you already have on hand, and the finished result is the same either way.

tools.note: For the through-holes that position the dowels.

materials.name: Closet rod dowel, 1-1/4", 8 in
materials.note: Alternative: hooks or 2x2 blocks work in place of dowels.

materials.name: Flat metal strip, 18 in, or pine 1x2
Wood screws, 2"
materials.note: The simplest assembly path — least amount of tools and hardware needed.

steps.title: Attach the shelf sides to the shelf
steps.body: Nail, screw, or pocket-hole the two shelf side pieces to the shelf board — whichever method matches the tools you already have on hand.

steps.title: Attach the shelf to the back panel
steps.body: From the back, attach the assembled shelf to the plywood back panel with glue and nails or screws, keeping all outside edges flush.

steps.title: Drill and install the dowels
steps.body: Predrill the dowel screw holes all the way through the plywood from the front, at the positions where you want the dowels. Flip the panel over, insert screws from the back, line up the dowels centered on the screw tips, and attach — the front holes give you an exact guide for driving each screw straight and centered into its dowel.

steps.title: Add the iron-hanging bar
steps.body: Attach the metal strip or 1x2 bar across the front near the top to hold the iron.
materials: Flat metal strip, 18 in, or pine 1x2

images.alt: Wall-mounted wooden holder for an ironing board with dowel pegs and an iron bar
